title: Motorola is allegedly working on a fourth FIFA World Cup 26 Edition phone
description: Motorola already launched the Razr 60 FIFA World Cup 26 Edition, and we recently heard that the company would also make FIFA World Cup 26 themed versions of both the Signature and the Razr Fold. Today the same source is back with another bombshell: Motorola is apparently working on a fourth World Cup special edition. There will also be the Motorola Edge 70 Fusion FIFA World Cup 26 Edition. Motorola Razr 60 FIFA World Cup 26 Edition Clearly then, Motorola is going all-in with its collaboration with FIFA for this year's World Cup, which is taking place in Canada, the US, and Mexico...

title: Exynos 2600 inside Galaxy S26 posts impressive Ray Tracing score
description: Samsung's Exynos 2600 SoC, which will power (at least) the base model Galaxy S26 models (in some regions, it remains to be seen), boasts the best Ray Tracing in the game. The Exynos 2600's graphics processor, the Xclipse 960, posted a chart-topping 8,321 points in Basemark's In Vitro 1.0's Ray Tracing test, beating out the Adreno 840 inside the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 by some 8%, and the Mali-G1-Ultra inside the MediaTek Dimensity 9500 by 17%. The Xclipse 960 uses AMD's latest RDNA 4 Ray Tracing technology, found in AMD's current-gen graphics cards. So if graphics are your...

title: Tecno Camon 50 and 50 Pro debut with Helio G200 chipset and 6,150mAh batteries
description: Tecno’s Camon 50 series was supposed to get unveiled at MWC next month, but the Camon 50 and Camon 50 Pro are already official in several markets in Africa. The pair share similar specs, but the Pro model does get a curved screen and a 3x telephoto camera. Tecno Camon 50 Pro (left) and Camon 50 (right) Camon 50 Pro is built around a curved 6.78" AMOLED with FHD+ resolution and a 144Hz refresh rate. The panel features a 32MP selfie camera and an under-display optical fingerprint scanner. The non-Pro model features the same screen specs, selfie cam and fingerprint scanner but opts...

title: Lava Bold N2 4G unveiled with a 5,000mAh battery, IP64 rating
description: Lava, the Indian smartphone brand, has launched the Bold N2 4G in the country. It is an affordable 4G handset available in two color options and costs less than $100. The Lava Bold N2 4G succeeds the Bold N1 4G, which was unveiled in June last year. While the new model shares many of its specs with its predecessor, it features a refreshed design and improved ingress protection. The phone is powered by an octa-core Unisoc SC9863A chipset, which is paired with 4GB of RAM and 64GB of internal storage. It does feature a microSD card slot for storage expansion. Lava has updated the...

title: Nothing confirms Phone (4a) series launch event
description: Nothing has been teasing its Phone (4a) series for a while now and we finally have a launch date to look forward to – March 5. Nothing CEO Carl Pei shared a poster that hijacks the event invite for Apple’s own March 4 special experience event with a bright pink spray-paint overlay. Nothing Phone (4a) series launch poster We’re expecting two phones from Nothing – the Phone (4a) and Phone (4a) Pro. The Phone (4a) series is rumored to be powered by the Snapdragon 7-series chipsets, with the vanilla model appearing in a Geekbench test run with the Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 chip. Nothing...

title: Infinix GT 50 Pro design and key specifications leaked
description: Infinix is expected to launch the successor to the GT 30 Pro soon. While the brand has yet to share official details, the Infinix GT 50 Pro was spotted on Geekbench last month. Now, additional information, including design renders, has leaked. The leaked renders reveal a gaming-focused design for the GT 50 Pro, featuring a carbon-fiber weave pattern on the rear panel along with LED accents at the bottom. The device will reportedly sport a 6.78-inch AMOLED display with a 1.5K resolution and a refresh rate of 144Hz. It is said to be powered by the MediaTek Dimensity 8400 Ultimate SoC,...

title: Galaxy S26 Ultra's selfie camera tipped to use a new sensor
description: Samsung is set to finally unveil the Galaxy S26 series next week at the Galaxy Unpacked event in San Francisco. Just days before the announcement, a tipster has shared new details about the Galaxy S26 Ultra. According to well-known tipster Ice Universe, the Galaxy S26 Ultra will feature an upgraded selfie camera. While it will retain a 12MP front-facing camera, matching the Galaxy S25 Ultra, the device will use a new sensor. Galaxy S26 Ultra leaked press image The Galaxy S26 Ultra is tipped to switch to a Sony sensor, replacing the Samsung ISOCELL 3LU used in its predecessor. Other...

title: Oppo Find X10 series said to offer MagSafe-like wireless charging
description: Oppo’s Find X10 series is still several months away from launch, but a new leak has revealed details about the phones’ charging capabilities. The lineup is expected to include the Find X10, Find X10 Pro, and Find X10 Pro Max. According to a tipster on Weibo, the upcoming Find X10 lineup could include built-in magnets to support MagSafe-style wireless charging. Oppo Find X9 Pro Outside of Google’s Pixel 10 series, no other Android smartphones offer the feature. Even Samsung’s upcoming Galaxy S26 lineup is rumored to skip built-in magnets and support only standard Qi2 wireless...

title: Infinix Note 60 Ultra FCC certification reveals two battery capacities
description: The upcoming Infinix Note 60 Ultra, designed by famed Italian house Pininfarina, has been certified by the FCC with the model number X6877. The certification interestingly reveals that the phone will have two battery capacities - rated at 5,850 mAh and 6,840 mAh, and advertised as typical 6,000 mAh and 7,000 mAh, respectively. This leads us to believe that, like other brands, Infinix will offer the Note 60 Ultra with the lower capacity in some markets like Europe, and with the higher one elsewhere. The FCC has also revealed that the Note 60 Ultra will support 30W wireless...

title: Poco X8 Pro runs Geekbench, it's powered by a new chipset
description: The Poco X8 Pro and Poco X8 Pro Max are definitely both coming soon, since we've already seen them getting certified by various authorities across the globe. Today, the Poco X8 Pro has been spotted in the Geekbench online database, because a prototype has run the benchmark. As you can see from the screenshot below, it managed a single-core score of 1,708 and a multi-core score of 6,297. It's powered by the recently unveiled MediaTek Dimensity 8500 SoC, the benchmark listing confirms. In the prototype that ran the benchmark, this was paired with 12GB of RAM, but of course Poco could...

title: Here's another look at the Galaxy Buds4 and Buds4 Pro ahead of launch next week
description: While the Galaxy S26 series will headline next week’s Galaxy Unpacked event, Samsung is also expected to unveil the Galaxy Buds4 and Galaxy Buds4 Pro earbuds. Ahead of the launch, new official-looking renders of the upcoming Galaxy earbuds have surfaced, courtesy of a tipster. The renders don’t really reveal anything new, but they offer the clearest look yet at the Galaxy Buds4 and Buds4 Pro, along with their charging cases. Samsung will include a charging cable and extra ear tips with the Pro variant, while the standard model will come with only a charging cable in the box. Both...

title: Xiaomi 17 and Xiaomi 17 Ultra global launch date leaks along with European prices
description: Two separate prior leaks told us that both the Xiaomi 17 and the Xiaomi 17 Ultra would be priced exactly the same as their predecessors in Europe, and now a new report from France comes to confirm this information. Xiaomi 17 Ultra The Xiaomi 17 and Xiaomi 17 Ultra pricing for the Eurozone is in the table below. Model Colors Configuration (RAM / Storage) Price Xiaomi 17 Black, Venture Green, Ice Blue, Alpine Pink 12 / 256 GB €999 12 / 512 GB €1,099 Xiaomi 17 Ultra Black, White,...

title: Samsung demos Galaxy S26 Ultra's privacy display in short video
description: Last month, Samsung teased that it will add “a new layer of privacy” to the Galaxy S26 Ultra with the new privacy screen feature. Now the company has shared a short video demonstrating how it works – it can make the whole screen or specific parts of it appear black when viewed from the side, without affecting legibility for you. Here’s the video. In this case the whole screen gets blacked out for any unwanted onlookers when the Zero-peeking privacy toggle is enabled. What’s interesting is that the phone has per-pixel control over this feature and can only black out notifications with...

title: vivo X300 FE gets more certifications, runs Geekbench and confirms its chipset
description: The vivo X300 FE was certified for sale in Europe back in October, and in the meantime it's been given the go-ahead in India by BIS as well. Now, IMDA in Singapore has certified it too, along with TÜV in Germany. The phone has the model number V2537. The TÜV certification reveals that the phone has support for 90W wired charging. The X300 FE has also run Geekbench, revealing that it's powered by the Snapdragon 8 Gen 5 SoC, paired with 12GB of RAM in the prototype that ran the benchmark. Of course, vivo could offer other RAM amounts too. The device will run Android 16 with...

title: Honor Magic V6 breaks cover ahead of MWC launch
description: Honor is actively preparing the teaser campaign for its next flagship foldable - the Magic V6. The device was spotted in the hands of actor Nicholas Tse, giving us our first live look at its design ahead of its debut at MWC Barcelona. The V6 is shown in its red colorway and it resembles its predecessor with an octagon-shaped camera island with red and gold accents. The V6 will feature the flat sides and will retain the fingerprint scanner embedded inside the power button. Honor Magic V6 design The Chinese version of the Magic V6 is expected to bring a 7,150mAh battery with...

title: Motorola Signature review
Apple schedules special experience event for March 4
description: Apple is holding its first event of the year on March 4. Cupertino will host a “special Apple experience” across New York, London and Shanghai, with the event scheduled to start at 9AM ET/ 2PM GMT/10PM CST time. It remains to be seen if the event will be livestreamed or if Apple will simply launch the products via press releases. Apple event invite We’re expecting several products, with the iPhone 17e being the most often rumored device in the last few weeks. The successor to last year’s iPhone 16e is rumored to get upgraded to the Apple A19 chipset alongside a 6.1-inch screen with...

title: Comments on YouTube videos are failing to load and anti-adblock measures may be to blame
description: YouTube is having trouble with loading the comment section under its videos – some think that this is an anti-adblock measure. Multiple users on Reddit are reporting encountering the issue. For some, the video description also fails to load, not just the comments. People have tried multiple workarounds, e.g. refreshing the page seems to work occasionally, which suggests that this is a regular site issue. However, the most reliable “workaround” discovered so far is turning off the ad blocker. Comments and video descriptions are failing to load for some users What’s curious is that...

title: IDC: Indian smartphone market remained flat in 2025
description: After a rocky start at the beginning of 2025, the Indian smartphone market recovered in mid-year and recorded a modest 0.5% year-over-year growth by the end, the latest IDC report claims. Manufacturers shipped 152 million units, and despite the market's flat state, some smartphone makers capitalized, mainly due to shifting consumer preferences. India's consumers are increasingly more interested in high-value smartphones, demanding premium and feature-rich models. This is evidenced by the 9% year-over-year value growth and the increased average selling prices, which rose 4% YoY to...

title: Snapdragon-powered Galaxy S26 Ultra smashes Exynos-powered S26 in single-core performance
description: The Samsung Galaxy S26 series will be unveiled on Wednesday next week (February 25). In case you missed them, here are our articles covering all the leaked info for the Galaxy S26 Ultra as well as the Galaxy S26 and S26+. Either way, you must have heard by now – the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra will use the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 in all regions, while the Galaxy S26 and S26+ will use the Exynos 2600 in several regions (the exact regions are not 100% clear at this point). Here’s a preview of what to expect from those chips, thanks to TechManiacs who have gathered system info and benchmark...
